dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won the day because HN commenters repeatedly treated its refusal to loosen military-use guardrails as a principled stand worth defending, even when that meant risking government business and political retaliation [E1][E2][E4][E9].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the clearest positive-throughline across multiple front-page threads: commenters described the company as principled, admirable, and willing to lose a seat at the table rather than surrender its safeguards [E1][E2]. That favorable contrast kept resurfacing in related Pentagon/OpenAI discussions, where users explicitly pointed to Anthropic's stance as the one they wanted to reward with their subscriptions [E4][E9].

openai.judgementSnippet: Backlash centered on OpenAI's Department of War agreement: commenters called the "all lawful purposes" framing too loose, worried it weakened practical red lines, canceled ChatGPT subscriptions, and echoed cronyism accusations in a separate thread [E3][E5][E6][E7].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Support clustered around Anthropic's insistence on stronger military-use safeguards; commenters called the stance admirable, urged the company to stay strong under pressure, and in comparison threads said that position was the one worth supporting [E1][E2][E4][E9].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: The Gemini CLI access thread was dominated by complaints about opaque bans, risky coupling to primary Google accounts, unclear terms around auth usage, and the absence of a credible appeal path [E8].

evidence.summary: In comments on "We Will Not Be Divided," users framed government pressure on Anthropic as an abuse of procurement power and sympathized with Anthropic as an important tool provider being punished for its stance.
Anthropic's statement thread drew overt praise, with top comments calling its position admirable, intelligent, principled, and worth supporting despite political pressure.
OpenAI's classified-network deal triggered accusations of opportunism and claims it would not really enforce red lines; multiple commenters said they were canceling subscriptions over it.
Within the OpenAI deal thread, commenters explicitly contrasted Anthropic favorably, saying its harder line on safeguards looked like the correct long-term approach.
OpenAI's own agreement post drew criticism that "all lawful purposes" was too permissive on surveillance and weaponization, especially when compared with Anthropic's stricter position.
The ChatGPT cancellation story became a venue for anti-OpenAI sentiment, including complaints about subscription handling and users saying recent behavior pushed them from ChatGPT toward Claude.
In "The whole thing was a scam," top comments amplified allegations of favoritism, bribery, and connected-party influence involving OpenAI, reinforcing a strongly hostile tone.
The Gemini CLI access/restoration discussion was dominated by complaints about unexplained bans, dangerous account-level consequences, confusing TOS changes, and no meaningful appeals process.
In the OpenAI agreement thread, commenters said they were canceling ChatGPT and trying Claude because Anthropic's stance better matched the message they wanted to send.
